links.title: Think Anywhere in Code Generation

primary_link.title: Think Anywhere in Code Generation

papers.summary: Recent advances in reasoning Large Language Models (LLMs) have primarily relied on upfront thinking, where reasoning occurs before final answer. However, this approach suffers from critical limitations in code generation, where upfront thinking is often insufficient as problems' full complexity only reveals itself during code implementation. Moreover, it cannot adaptively allocate reasoning effort throughout the code generation process where difficulty varies significantly. In this paper, we propose Think-Anywhere, a novel reasoning mechanism that enables LLMs to invoke thinking on-demand at any token position during code generation. We achieve Think-Anywhere by first teaching LLMs to imitate the reasoning patterns through cold-start training, then leveraging outcome-based RL rewards to drive the model's autonomous exploration of when and where to invoke reasoning. Extensive experiments on four mainstream code generation benchmarks (i.e., LeetCode, LiveCodeBench, HumanEval, and MBPP) show that Think-Anywhere achieves state-of-the-art performance over both existing reasoning methods and recent post-training approaches, while demonstrating consistent generalization across diverse LLMs. Our analysis further reveals that Think-Anywhere enables the model to adaptively invoke reasoning at high-entropy positions, providing enhanced interpretability.
papers.summary_zh: 近期在推理型 Large Language Models (LLMs) 方面的進展，主要依賴於預先思考 (upfront thinking)，即推理發生在得出最終答案之前。然而，這種方法在程式碼生成中存在嚴重的局限性，因為問題的完整複雜性通常只在程式碼實作過程中才會顯現，導致預先思考往往不足。此外，它無法在程式碼生成過程中，根據難度顯著變化的情況，自適應地分配推理努力。在本文中，我們提出了 Think-Anywhere，這是一種新穎的推理機制，使 LLMs 能夠在程式碼生成期間的任何 token 位置按需調用思考。我們透過首先透過 cold-start training 教導 LLMs 模仿推理模式，然後利用基於結果的 RL rewards 來驅動模型自主探索何時何地調用推理，從而實現 Think-Anywhere。在四個主流程式碼生成基準測試 (即 LeetCode, LiveCodeBench, HumanEval, 和 MBPP) 上的大量實驗表明，Think-Anywhere 在現有推理方法和近期 post-training 方法上均實現了 state-of-the-art 性能，同時在各種 LLMs 中展現出一致的泛化能力。我們的分析進一步揭示，Think-Anywhere 使模型能夠在高熵位置自適應地調用推理，從而提供增強的可解釋性。
papers.title: Think Anywhere in Code Generation
papers.title_zh: 在程式碼生成中隨處思考 (Think Anywhere)

links.title: FlowPIE: Test-Time Scientific Idea Evolution with Flow-Guided Literature Exploration

primary_link.title: FlowPIE: Test-Time Scientific Idea Evolution with Flow-Guided Literature Exploration

papers.summary: Scientific idea generation (SIG) is critical to AI-driven autonomous research, yet existing approaches are often constrained by a static retrieval-then-generation paradigm, leading to homogeneous and insufficiently divergent ideas. In this work, we propose FlowPIE, a tightly coupled retrieval-generation framework that treats literature exploration and idea generation as a co-evolving process. FlowPIE expands literature trajectories via a flow-guided Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) inspired by GFlowNets, using the quality of current ideas assessed by an LLM-based generative reward model (GRM) as a supervised signal to guide adaptive retrieval and construct a diverse, high-quality initial population. Based on this population, FlowPIE models idea generation as a test-time idea evolution process, applying selection, crossover, and mutation with the isolation island paradigm and GRM-based fitness computation to incorporate cross-domain knowledge. It effectively mitigates the information cocoons arising from over-reliance on parametric knowledge and static literature. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that FlowPIE consistently produces ideas with higher novelty, feasibility and diversity compared to strong LLM-based and agent-based frameworks, while enabling reward scaling during test time.
papers.summary_zh: 科學思想生成 (Scientific Idea Generation, SIG) 對於 AI 驅動的自主研究至關重要，然而現有方法常受限於靜態的「檢索-然後生成」範式，導致思想同質且發散性不足。在這項工作中，我們提出了 FlowPIE，這是一個緊密耦合的檢索-生成框架，將文獻探索和思想生成視為一個共同演化的過程。FlowPIE 透過受 GFlowNets 啟發的 flow-guided Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) 擴展文獻軌跡，利用由 LLM-based generative reward model (GRM) 評估的當前思想品質作為監督訊號，以指導自適應檢索並構建多樣化、高品質的初始群體。基於這個群體，FlowPIE 將思想生成建模為一個測試時的思想演化過程，運用隔離島範式 (isolation island paradigm) 和 GRM-based fitness computation 進行選擇、交叉和突變，以整合跨領域知識。它有效緩解了過度依賴參數知識和靜態文獻所導致的資訊繭房。廣泛的評估表明，相較於強大的 LLM-based 和 agent-based 框架，FlowPIE 始終產生更高新穎性、可行性和多樣性的思想，同時在測試時能實現 reward scaling。
papers.title: FlowPIE: Test-Time Scientific Idea Evolution with Flow-Guided Literature Exploration
papers.title_zh: FlowPIE：在測試時透過流程引導的文獻探索實現科學思想演化

links.title: SeGPruner: Semantic-Geometric Visual Token Pruner for 3D Question Answering

primary_link.title: SeGPruner: Semantic-Geometric Visual Token Pruner for 3D Question Answering

papers.summary: Vision-language models (VLMs) have been widely adopted for 3D question answering (3D QA). In typical pipelines, visual tokens extracted from multiple viewpoints are concatenated with language tokens and jointly processed by a large language model (LLM) for inference. However, aggregating multi-view observations inevitably introduces severe token redundancy, leading to an overly large visual token set that significantly hinders inference efficiency under constrained token budgets. Visual token pruning has emerged as a prevalent strategy to address this issue. Nevertheless, most existing pruners are primarily tailored to 2D inputs or rely on indirect geometric cues, which limits their ability to explicitly retain semantically critical objects and maintain sufficient spatial coverage for robust 3D reasoning. In this paper, we propose SeGPruner, a semantic-aware and geometry-guided token reduction framework for efficient 3D QA with multi-view images. Specifically, SeGPruner first preserves semantically salient tokens through an attention-based importance module (Saliency-aware Token Selector), ensuring that object-critical evidence is retained. It then complements these tokens with spatially diverse ones via a geometry-guided selector (Geometry-aware Token Diversifier), which jointly considers semantic relevance and 3D geometric distance. This cooperation between saliency preservation and geometry-guided diversification balances object-level evidence and global scene coverage under aggressive token reduction. Extensive experiments on ScanQA and OpenEQA demonstrate that SeGPruner substantially improves inference efficiency, reducing the visual token budget by 91% and inference latency by 86%, while maintaining competitive performance in 3D reasoning tasks.
papers.summary_zh: Vision-language models (VLMs) 已被廣泛應用於 3D question answering (3D QA)。在典型的 pipelines 中，從多個視角提取的 visual tokens 與 language tokens 串聯，並由 large language model (LLM) 共同處理以進行 inference。然而，聚合多視角觀測不可避免地會引入嚴重的 token redundancy，導致視覺 token 集過大，在受限的 token budgets 下顯著阻礙 inference 效率。Visual token pruning 已成為解決此問題的普遍策略。然而，大多數現有的 pruners 主要針對 2D inputs，或依賴間接的 geometric cues，這限制了它們明確保留語義關鍵物件並保持足夠空間覆蓋以進行穩健 3D reasoning 的能力。在本文中，我們提出了 SeGPruner，這是一個語義感知且幾何引導的 token reduction 框架，用於透過多視角圖像實現高效 3D QA。具體而言，SeGPruner 首先透過基於 attention 的重要性模組 (Saliency-aware Token Selector) 保留語義顯著的 tokens，確保保留物件關鍵證據。然後，它透過幾何引導選擇器 (Geometry-aware Token Diversifier) 補充這些 tokens 以獲得空間多樣性，該選擇器共同考慮語義相關性和 3D geometric distance。這種顯著性保留與幾何引導多樣化之間的合作，在激進的 token reduction 下平衡了物件級證據和全局場景覆蓋。在 ScanQA 和 OpenEQA 上的廣泛實驗證明，SeGPruner 顯著提高了 inference 效率，將 visual token budget 減少了 91%，inference latency 減少了 86%，同時在 3D reasoning 任務中保持了競爭性能。
papers.title: SeGPruner: Semantic-Geometric Visual Token Pruner for 3D Question Answering
papers.title_zh: SeGPruner: Semantic-Geometric Visual Token Pruner for 3D Question Answering

links.title: Video Models Reason Early: Exploiting Plan Commitment for Maze Solving

primary_link.title: Video Models Reason Early: Exploiting Plan Commitment for Maze Solving

papers.summary: Video diffusion models exhibit emergent reasoning capabilities like solving mazes and puzzles, yet little is understood about how they reason during generation. We take a first step towards understanding this and study the internal planning dynamics of video models using 2D maze solving as a controlled testbed. Our investigations reveal two findings. Our first finding is early plan commitment: video diffusion models commit to a high-level motion plan within the first few denoising steps, after w
papers.summary_zh: 影像 diffusion models 展現出 emergent 的推理能力，例如解決迷宮和拼圖，但對於它們在生成過程中如何推斷卻知之甚少。我們邁出了理解這方面的重要一步，並以 2D 迷宮求解作為受控測試平台，研究了影像模型的內部規劃動態。我們的調查揭示了兩個發現。我們第一個發現是 early plan commitment：影像 diffusion models 在最初的幾個 denoising steps 內，會確定一個高層次的運動計劃，在 w 之後。

papers.summary: This study investigates the use of NeuralUCB for cost-aware large language model (LLM) routing. papers.summary_zh: 類比推理是人類在問題解決和論證中進行泛化的關鍵驅動力。然而，敘事結構之間的類比對於機器來說仍然具有挑戰性。用於 structural mapping 的 cognitive engines 並不直接適用，因為它們假定預先提取的實體，而 LLMs 的性能對 prompt format 以及敘事之間表面相似性的程度很敏感。這個差距引出了一個關鍵問題：利用 LLM-derived 的抽象化增強 structural mapping 的影響是什麼？

SceneTeract decomposes complex activities into sequences of atomic actions and valida
papers.summary_zh: Embodied AI 依賴於支援不同用戶有意義活動的互動式 3D 環境，然而評估其 functional affordances 仍然是一個核心挑戰。我們引入了 SceneTeract，這是一個在 agent-specific 約束下驗證 3D 場景功能的框架。我們的核心貢獻是一個 grounded verification engine，它將高層次 semantic reasoning 與低層次 geometric checks 相結合。SceneTeract 將複雜活動分解為原子動作序列並進行驗證。

papers.summary: Although many AI applications of interest require specialized multi-modal models, relevant data to train such models is inherently scarce or inaccessible. papers.summary: Recent advances in vision-language models (VLMs) have accelerated their application to indoor safety hazards assessment. papers.summary_zh: 我們解決了將預訓練 Large Language Models (LLMs) 用於多變量 Time-Series analysis 的挑戰，其中它們的部署常因過高的計算和記憶體需求而受阻。我們的解決方案 One-for-All 引入了 Gaussian Rank-Stabilized Low-Rank Adapters (rsLoRA)，以實現 frozen LLMs 的 parameter-efficient fine-tuning。儘管受到 LoRA 的啟發，rsLoRA 引入了一種具有數學基礎的 rank-stabilization 機制，可以在低 rank 情況下實現可證明的 gradient stability。

links.title: CutClaw: Agentic Hours-Long Video Editing via Music Synchronization

primary_link.title: CutClaw: Agentic Hours-Long Video Editing via Music Synchronization

papers.summary: Editing the video content with audio alignment forms a digital human-made art in current social media. However, the time-consuming and repetitive nature of manual video editing has long been a challenge for filmmakers and professional content creators alike. In this paper, we introduce CutClaw, an autonomous multi-agent framework designed to edit hours-long raw footage into meaningful short videos that leverages the capabilities of multiple Multimodal Language Models~(MLLMs) as an agent system. It produces videos with synchronized music, followed by instructions, and a visually appealing appearance. In detail, our approach begins by employing a hierarchical multimodal decomposition that captures both fine-grained details and global structures across visual and audio footage. Then, to ensure narrative consistency, a Playwriter Agent orchestrates the whole storytelling flow and structures the long-term narrative, anchoring visual scenes to musical shifts. Finally, to construct a short edited video, Editor and Reviewer Agents collaboratively optimize the final cut via selecting fine-grained visual content based on rigorous aesthetic and semantic criteria. We conduct detailed experiments to demonstrate that CutClaw significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in generating high-quality, rhythm-aligned videos. The code is available at: https://github.com/GVCLab/CutClaw.
papers.summary_zh: 在當前的社群媒體中，結合音訊對齊來編輯影片內容形成了一種數位人工藝術。然而，手動影片編輯耗時且重複的特性長期以來一直是電影製作人和專業內容創作者的挑戰。在本文中，我們介紹了 CutClaw，這是一個自主的 multi-agent 框架，旨在將數小時的原始素材編輯成有意義的短影片，它利用了多個 Multimodal Language Models (MLLMs) 作為 agent 系統的能力。它產生的影片具有同步的音樂、遵循指示，並具有視覺吸引力。具體而言，我們的方法首先採用分層的 multimodal decomposition，捕捉視覺和音訊素材中的細粒度細節和全域結構。然後，為了確保敘事的一致性，一個 Playwriter Agent 協調整個故事流程並建構長期敘事，將視覺場景錨定到音樂的變化上。最後，為了建構一個經過編輯的短影片，Editor 和 Reviewer Agents 透過基於嚴格的美學和語義標準選擇細粒度的視覺內容，協同優化最終剪輯。我們進行了詳細的實驗，證明 CutClaw 在生成高品質、節奏對齊的影片方面顯著優於現有的 state-of-the-art 基線。程式碼可在此處取得：https://github.com/GVCLab/CutClaw。
papers.title: CutClaw: Agentic Hours-Long Video Editing via Music Synchronization
papers.title_zh: CutClaw：透過音樂同步實現的 Agentic 長時影片編輯

To address this issue, we introduce Minimum Spanning Tree Compactness (MST-C), a graph-based structural metric that captures higher-order geometric properties of attributions, such as spread and cohesion. papers.summary_zh: 在歸因品質評估中，解釋可讀性的定量評估尤其困難，因為它受到歸因的各種形狀和內部組織的影響，而這些是簡單統計數據無法捕捉的。為了解決這個問題，我們引入了最小生成樹緊湊性 (Minimum Spanning Tree Compactness, MST-C)，這是一種基於圖的結構度量，它能捕捉歸因的高階幾何特性，例如分佈和凝聚力。這些成分被組合成一個單一的分數，該分數能有效評估解釋的品質。

links.title: AWS launches frontier agents for security testing and cloud operations

primary_link.title: AWS launches frontier agents for security testing and cloud operations

radar.summary: I'm excited to announce that AWS Security Agent on-demand penetration testing and AWS DevOps Agent are now generally available, representing a new class of AI capabilities we announced at re:Invent called frontier agents. These autonomous systems work independently to achieve goals, scale massively to tackle concurrent tasks, and run persistently for hours or days without constant human oversight. Together, these agents are changing the way we secure and operate software. In preview, customers and partners report that AWS Security Agent compresses penetration testing timelines from weeks to hours and the AWS DevOps Agent supports 3–5x faster incident resolution.

links.title: Unify-Agent: A Unified Multimodal Agent for World-Grounded Image Synthesis

primary_link.title: Unify-Agent: A Unified Multimodal Agent for World-Grounded Image Synthesis

top5.summary: Unified multimodal models provide a natural and promising architecture for understanding diverse and complex real-world knowledge while generating high-quality images. However, they still rely primarily on frozen parametric knowledge, which makes them struggle with real-world image generation involving long-tail and knowledge-intensive concepts. Inspired by the broad success of agents on real-world tasks, we explore agentic modeling to address this limitation. Specifically, we present Unify-Agent, a unified multimodal agent for world-grounded image synthesis, which reframes image generation as an agentic pipeline consisting of prompt understanding, multimodal evidence searching, grounded recaptioning, and final synthesis. To train our model, we construct a tailored multimodal data pipeline and curate 143K high-quality agent trajectories for world-grounded image synthesis, enabling effective supervision over the full agentic generation process. We further introduce FactIP, a benchmark covering 12 categories of culturally significant and long-tail factual concepts that explicitly requires external knowledge grounding. Extensive experiments show that our proposed Unify-Agent substantially improves over its base unified model across diverse benchmarks and real world generation tasks, while approaching the world knowledge capabilities of the strongest closed-source models. As an early exploration of agent-based modeling for world-grounded image synthesis, our work highlights the value of tightly coupling reasoning, searching, and generation for reliable open-world agentic image synthesis.
top5.summary_zh: 統一多模態模型為理解多樣且複雜的現實世界知識並生成高品質圖像，提供了一種自然且有前景的架構。然而，它們仍主要依賴於 frozen parametric knowledge，這使得它們難以處理涉及 long-tail 和 knowledge-intensive 概念的現實世界圖像生成。受 agents 在現實世界任務中廣泛成功的啟發，我們探索 agentic modeling 來解決這一限制。具體來說，我們提出了 Unify-Agent，一種用於世界落地圖像合成的統一多模態 agent，它將圖像生成重新定義為一個 agentic pipeline，包括 prompt understanding、multimodal evidence searching、grounded recaptioning 和最終的 synthesis。為了訓練我們的模型，我們構建了一個量身定制的 multimodal data pipeline，並策劃了 143K 條高品質的 agent trajectory，用於世界落地圖像合成，從而實現對完整 agentic generation 過程的有效監督。我們進一步引入了 FactIP，這是一個基準測試，涵蓋 12 類具有文化意義和 long-tail 的事實概念，明確要求 external knowledge grounding。大量實驗表明，我們提出的 Unify-Agent 在各種基準測試和現實世界生成任務中，相較於其基礎統一模型有顯著改進，同時也接近最強大的 closed-source models 的世界知識能力。作為對世界落地圖像合成的基於 agent 的建模的早期探索，我們的工作強調了緊密結合 reasoning、searching 和 generation 對於可靠的 open-world agentic image synthesis 的價值。

top5.title_zh: Unify-Agent：一種用於世界落地圖像合成的統一多模態 Agent
